title: Descurainia tenuissima
taxonomy: Descurainia tenuissima is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. This family is commonly known as the mustard or cabbage family, which includes a wide variety of plants such as broccoli, cauliflower, and kale. Descurainia tenuissima is a member of the genus Descurainia, which is a group of plants native to North America. The genus Descurainia is further classified as being part of the tribe Brassiceae, which is a subfamily of the Brassicaceae family. Descurainia tenuissima is a perennial plant that grows in dry areas and is often found in sandy or rocky soils.
uses: Descurainia tenuissima, also known as Torn-mussel or slender rockcress, has been used in traditional medicine. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
